chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The supplication in the road

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/182)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/182)
text: قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ لِنَفْسِيَ الْيَقِينَ وَ الْعَفْوَ وَ الْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ ثِقَتِي وَ أَنْتَ رَجَائِي وَ أَنْتَ عَضُدِي وَ أَنْتَ نَاصِرِي بِكَ أَحُلُّ وَ بِكَ أَسِيرُ
قَالَ وَ مَنْ يَخْرُجْ فِي سَفَرٍ وَحْدَهُ فَلْيَقُلْ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ اللَّهُمَّ آنِسْ وَحْشَتِي وَ أَعِنِّي عَلَى وَحْدَتِي وَ أَدِّ غَيْبَتِي .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Aban Bin Usman, from Isa Bin Abdullah Al qummi,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Say, ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I ask You<sup>azwj</sup> for the conviction for myself, and the Forgiveness, and the good health in the world and the Hereafter. O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! You<sup>azwj</sup> are my Reliable One, and You<sup>azwj</sup> are my Hope, and You<sup>azwj</sup> are my backbone, and You<sup>azwj</sup> are my Helper. With You<sup>azwj</sup> I disembark and with You<sup>azwj</sup> do I travel’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘And the one who goes out alone in a journey, so let him say, ‘Whatever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> so Desires. There is no Strength except by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Comfort me in my fear, and Support me upon my loneliness, and End my absence (from home)’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from ‘Isa ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Qummiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that you should say, ‘O Allah, I appeal before You to grant my soul certainty, forgiveness and good health in this life and in the next life. O Allah, You are my trustee, my hope, my shoulder (power) and my supporter. With Your help I disembark and travel.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘If one travels alone he should say, ‘Whatever Allah wills happens. There is no power without the help of Allah. O Allah, comfort me in my fear, help me in my loneliness and make the end of my absence (from my home) closer.’”
